I cannot provide a definitive answer without the specific requirement and test cases you are referring to. However, based on the provided information, it appears that the system is designed to handle various types of computers, tablets, and smartphones, with different architectures and operating systems for the user interface. The tests mentioned in the input aim to verify that the system can correctly handle the user interface with these devices and systems. The tests include verifying that all relevant information is logged in the security log, verifying that the Metering Units can be configured for regular automatic collection of Measurement values, detecting meters with communication problems, and verifying that the Metering Units can handle both primary and secondary registration of Metering Values. Additionally, the system is expected to be able to handle the installation and removal of metering units, as well as updates to the user interface and communication links. The tests also aim to ensure that the user interface response times do not exceed certain thresholds and that the system complies with certain regulations.

The following are test cases that cover the requirement:

1. Test case T-0: The requirement states that the communication solution includes licensed telecommunications networks and must comply with Ordinages and Regulations in accordance with PTSFS 2015:2. This requirement is covered by test cases T-5 and T-25. Test case T-5 verifies that the Metering Unit can be configured for regular automatic collection of Measurement values from Metering Units to HES, and Test case T-25 verifies that the licensed telecommunications network complies with Ordinages and Regulations in accordance with PTSFS 2015:2.

2. Test case T-1: The requirement states that the communication solution must have a security log. Test case T-1 covers this requirement by verifying that all relevant information is logged in the security log.

3. Test case T-2: The requirement states that the communication solution must be able to switch on and off and check the status of switches. Test case T-2 covers this requirement by verifying that switching on is not possible by integration from other systems, and that switching off is not possible by integration.

4. Test case T-3: The requirement states that the communication solution must be able to send and receive the same information from the file and meter. Test case T-3 covers this requirement by verifying that the Metering Unit can send and receive the same information from the file and meter.

5. Test case T-4: The requirement states that the Metering Unit has a life expectancy of 15 years from the date of delivery to GE. Test case T-4 covers this requirement by verifying that the Metering Unit can be installed with a life expectancy of 15 years.

6. Test case T-6: The requirement states that the Contractor must use a tool designated by the Contracting Authority to follow the Contracting Authority's reporting of test results. Test case T-6 covers this requirement by verifying that the Contractor uses a tool designated by the Contracting Authority to follow the Contracting Authority's reporting of test results.

7. Test case T-7: The purchaser's UAT (User Acceptance Test) must include testing and verification of all requirements of the Specification. Test case T-7 covers this requirement by verifying that the purchaser's UAT includes testing and verification of all requirements of the Specification.

8. Test case T-8: The Contractor must carry out the necessary training and submit the relevant documentation according to point 5 before the start of the Customer's UAT. Test case T-8 covers this requirement by verifying that the Contractor carries out the necessary training and submits the relevant documentation according to point 5 before the start of the Customer's UAT.

9. Test case T-9: The Metering Units must be able to install new Metering Units - Cat 1, 1 phase, Cat 1, 3 phase, Cat 2, and take down meter type Cat 1, 1 phase, Cat 1, 3 phase, Cat 2. Test case T-9 covers this requirement by verifying that the Metering Units can install new Metering Units - Cat 1, 1 phase, Cat 1, 3 phase, Cat 2, and take down meter type Cat 1, 1 phase, Cat 1, 3 phase, Cat 2.

10. Test case T-10: The Metering System must be able to record communication errors. Test case T-10 covers this requirement by verifying that the Metering System is able to record and store information when the following Events occur: communication failure.

11. Test case T-11: The Metering System must be able to detect and see individual Metering Units that have communication problems. Test case T-11 covers this requirement by verifying that in HES it is possible for the User to detect and see individual Metering Units that have communication problems.

12. Test case T-12: The Metering System must be able to display PQ values. Test case T-12 covers this requirement by verifying that in HES it is possible to analyze and visualize PQ values in e.g. graphs and bars.

13. Test case T-13: The Metering System must be able to copy static fields. Test case T-13 covers this requirement by verifying that the Metering System is able to copy static fields such as headers, status, responses to Events in the system.

14. Test case T-14: The user must be given the opportunity to request further confirmation before executing certain commands. Test case T-14 covers this requirement by verifying that commands which may have serious consequences in case of incorrect handling (deletion of stored measurement and structure data and switching off and on of power) require additional confirmation before being executed. Commands which do not have serious consequences do not require additional confirmation before being executed.

15. Test case T-15: The Metering Unit must have a life expectancy of 15 years from the date of delivery to GE. Test case T-15 covers this requirement by verifying that the Metering Unit has a life expectancy of 15 years from the date of delivery to GE.

16. Test case T-16: The Metering Unit for each phase must be able to measure and record the minimum, maximum and average value of voltage, current, active and reactive power of the electricity output and input per 5 minutes. Test case T-16 covers this requirement by verifying that the Metering Unit for each phase is able to measure and record the minimum, maximum and average value of voltage, current, active and reactive power of the electricity output and input per 5 minutes.

17. Test case T-17: The Metering System must be able to record communication errors. Test case T-17 covers this requirement by verifying that the Metering System is able to record and store information when the following Events occur: communication failure.

18. Test case T-18: In case of connection overheating, the communication link from the meter is disconnected. Test case T-18 covers this requirement by verifying that the Metering System is able to record and store information when the following Events occur: connection overheating.

19. Test case T-19: The Customer Interface in a Metering Unit must be enabled or disabled for data sending. Test case T-19 covers this requirement by verifying that in HES it is possible to see if the Customer Interface in a Metering Unit is enabled or disabled for data sending.

20. Test case T-20: The response times in the user interface must not exceed 0.5 seconds for input, keystroke or equivalent to first response on screen, 0.5 seconds for display of form view including data, 5 seconds for viewing simple report, and 15 seconds for login. Test case T-20 covers this requirement by verifying that the response times in the user interface do not exceed: 0.5 seconds for input, keystroke or equivalent to first response on screen, 0.5 seconds for display of form view including data, 5 seconds for viewing simple report, and 15 seconds for login.

21. Test case T-21: The Metering Units must be able to handle both primary and secondary registration - Cat2. Test case T-21 covers this requirement by verifying that the Metering Units can handle both primary and secondary registration - Cat2.

22. Test case T-22: The sealing device of the Metering Unit must be raised and clearly visible for smooth and accessible passage of the sealing wire. Test case T-22 covers this requirement by verifying that the sealing device of the Metering Unit is raised and clearly visible for smooth and accessible passage of the sealing wire.

23. Test case T-23: The sealing device of the Metering Unit must be raised and clearly visible for smooth and accessible passage of the sealing wire. Test case T-23 covers this requirement by verifying that the sealing device of the Metering Unit is raised and clearly visible for smooth and accessible passage of the sealing wire.

24. Test case T-24: The Metering Units must be able to handle both primary and secondary registration - Cat1. Test case T-24 covers this requirement by verifying that the Metering Units can handle both primary and secondary registration - Cat1.

25. Test case T-25: The licensed telecommunications network must comply with Ordinages and Regulations in accordance with PTSFS 2015:2 issued by PTS (Swedish Post and Telecom Authority). Test case T-25 covers this requirement by verifying that the licensed telecommunications network complies with Ordinages and Regulations in accordance with PTSFS 2015:2 issued by PTS (Swedish Post and Telecom Authority).

I cannot directly respond with code blocks, but I can provide you with a list of test IDs that cover the requirement based on the given information. Here's the list:

- T-0, T-1, T-2, T-3, T-4, T-5, T-6, T-7, T-8, T-11, T-12, T-13, T-14, T-15, T-16, T-17, T-18, T-19, T-20, T-21, T-22, T-23, T-24, T-25, T-26, T-27, T-28, T-29, T-30, T-31, T-32, T-33, T-34, T-35, T-36, T-37, T-38.

These tests cover the requirement of recording and storing information when communication failures occur, as well as testing the status of the customer interface and response times in the user interface. Additionally, the tests cover the installation and removal of metering units, as well as updating and setting up new metering units. The tests also ensure that the metering units can handle primary and secondary registration, as well as licensed telecommunications networks, and comply with Ordinages and Regulations. The tests also cover the management of both primary and secondary registration, sealing devices, and licensing requirements for telecommunications networks.

The following are test cases that cover the requirement:

1. Test case T-0: The requirement for logging data in the security log is covered by test cases T-0 and T-1. Test case T-0 verifies that all relevant information is logged in the security log and test case T-1 verifies that the log date and system time are recorded correctly.

2. Test case T-2: The requirement for switching on and off and status of breakers from other GE systems is covered by test case T-2. Test case T-2 verifies that switching on is not possible by integration from other systems and that switching off is not possible by integration. It also verifies that the status of switches is visible by integration.

3. Test case T-3: The requirement for automatic collection of measurement values is covered by test case T-3. Test case T-3 verifies that the metering system can be configured for regular automatic collection of measurement values from Metering Units to HES.

4. Test case T-5: The requirement for dependencies with other systems is covered by test case T-5. Test case T-5 verifies that the connection between Collection Manager and OMNIA Flex is turned off.

5. Test case T-6: The requirement for testing tools is covered by test case T-6. Test case T-6 verifies that the Contractor uses a tool designated by the Contracting Authority to follow the Contracting Authority's reporting of test results, the Supplier is responsible for registering in the tool its feedback and status on the Customer's reported errors and comments, and the Customer currently uses ReQtest in the defect management process.

6. Test case T-7: The requirement for scope of UAT is covered by test case T-7. Test case T-7 verifies that the purchaser's UAT (User Acceptance Test) includes testing and verification of all requirements of the Specification unless otherwise agreed.

7. Test case T-8: The requirement for training for UAT is covered by test case T-8. Test case T-8 verifies that the Contractor carries out the necessary training and submits the relevant documentation according to point 5 before the start of the Customer's UAT.

8. Test case T-9: The requirement for Metering units in HES is covered by test case T-9. Test case T-9 verifies that it is possible to install new Metering Unit - Cat 1, 1 phase, Cat 1, 3 phase, Cat 2, Cat 1, 1 phase, Cat 1, 3 phase, Cat 2, Cat 1, 1 phase, Cat 1, 3 phase, Cat 2 in HES and take down the existing meters.

9. Test case T-10: The requirement for automatic collection of Measured values is covered by test case T-10. Test case T-10 verifies that the metering system can be configured for regular automatic collection of measurement values from Metering Units to HES.

10. Test case T-11: The requirement for detecting meters with communication problems is covered by test case T-11. Test case T-11 verifies that in HES it is possible for the User to detect and see individual Metering Units that have communication problems.

11. Test case T-12: The requirement for displaying PQ values is covered by test case T-12. Test case T-12 verifies that in HES it is possible to analyze and visualize PQ values in e.g. graphs and bars.

12. Test case T-13: The requirement for copying static fields is covered by test case T-13. Test case T-13 verifies that it is possible to copy static fields in HES such as headers, status, responses to Events in the system.

13. Test case T-14: The requirement for requesting further confirmation is covered by test case T-14. Test case T-14 verifies that commands, initiated by the User in HES, which may have serious consequences in case of incorrect handling (deletion of stored measurement and structure data and switching off and on of power) require additional confirmation before being executed. Commands which do not have serious consequences do not require additional confirmation before being executed.

14. Test case T-15: The requirement for sealing devices is covered by test case T-15. Test case T-15 verifies that the sealing device of the Metering Unit is raised and clearly visible for smooth and accessible passage of the sealing wire.

16. Test case T-16: The requirement for communication errors is covered by test case T-16. Test case T-16 verifies that the Metering System is able to record and store information when the following Events occur: communication failure.

17. Test case T-17: The requirement for connection overheating is covered by test case T-17. Test case T-17 verifies that the Metering System is able to record and store information when the following Events occur: in case of connection overheating.

18. Test case T-18: The requirement for the customer interface status is covered by test case T-18. Test case T-18 verifies that in HES it is possible to see if the Customer Interface in a Metering Unit is enabled or disabled for data sending.

19. Test case T-20: The requirement for response times in the user interface is covered by test case T-20. Test case T-20 verifies that HES user interface response times do not exceed: - 0.5 seconds from input, keystroke or equivalent to first response on screen - 0.5 seconds for display of form view including data - 5 seconds for viewing simple report - 15 seconds for login.

20. Test case T-21: The requirement for management of both primary and secondary registration - Cat2 is covered by test case T-21. Test case T-21 verifies that the Metering Units can handle both primary and secondary recording of Metering Values.

22. Test case T-22: The requirement for sealing devices - Cat1 is covered by test case T-22. Test case T-22 verifies that the sealing device of the Metering Unit is raised and clearly visible for smooth and accessible passage of the sealing wire.

23. Test case T-23: The requirement for sealing devices - Cat2 is covered by test case T-23. Test case T-23 verifies that the sealing device of the Metering Unit is raised and clearly visible for smooth and accessible passage of the sealing wire.

24. Test case T-24: The requirement for management of both primary and secondary registration - Cat1 is covered by test case T-24. Test case T-24 verifies that the Metering Units can handle both primary and secondary recording of Metering Values.

25. Test case T-25: The requirement for licensed telecommunications networks is covered by test case T-25. Test case T-25 verifies that the licensed telecommunications network complies with Ordinances and Regulations in accordance with PTSFS 2015:2 issued by PTS (Post-och Telestyrelsen).

26. Test case T-26: The requirement for lifetimes of metering units is covered by test case T-26. A Metering Unit shall have a life expectancy of 15 years from the date of delivery to GE.

27. Test case T-27: The requirement for recovery time after communication breakdown is covered by test case T-27. Test case T-27 verifies that the communication link is broken and the voltage is left in the meter. Wait one hour and turn on the link again.

28. Test case T-28: The requirement for installation of new metering devices via integration is covered by test case T-28. Information regarding structure data for new set-up is sent from the integration interface Mint to Collection Manager. Verify that the information entered on the user point in Collection Manager corresponds to what is specified in the Work Order. An acknowledgment is sent to Generis that the meter is set up in Collection Manager. Check that the user point is displayed under the correct coordinates in the map view in Vision. Check that the meter has the correct profile and that Energy values, PQ values and GSM diagnostics are pushed from the meter at the correct interval.

29. Test case T-29: The requirement for dismantling of metering units is covered by test case T-29. Information regarding the structure data for the removal of a metering unit is sent from the integration interface Mint to Collection Manager. Verify that the metering unit is removed in Collection Manager and does not have a connection to the user point.

30. Test case T-30: The requirement for update of measurement point in CM is covered by test case T-30. Information regarding the structure data for updating a metering unit is sent from the integration interface Mint to Collection Manager. Verify that the information to be updated via Mint has also been changed at the current user point.
